title: camissonia claviformis subsp. funerea
intro: Camissonia claviformis subsp. funerea, also known as the Death Camas or Funereal Camissonia, is a rare and endangered species of flowering plant native to California. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making it useful for treating various health conditions. The health benefits of Camissonia claviformis subsp. funerea include reducing inflammation and fighting off infections, as well as possibly aiding in the treatment of respiratory issues and skin conditions. However, due to its toxicity and potential risks, it is not commonly used in herbal preparations and should be avoided, with some preparations including the use of the plant as a tincture or salve, but under the guidance of a medical professional.
